narrative:
## State of the Story: U.S.-Israel Military Operation Against Iran

### Overview
A major U.S.-Israel military operation against Iran, designated "Operation Epic Fury," began Saturday and is now in its third day [1][2]. President Trump estimated the conflict could last "about four weeks" and stated the U.S. is "a little ahead of schedule" [1].

### Military Casualties and Developments
Six U.S. service members have been confirmed killed in action [2][3]. The casualties were announced in stages: three deaths were initially confirmed Sunday [4], with two additional deaths confirmed Monday when U.S. Central Command recovered remains from a facility struck during Iranian retaliation [2]. Four additional U.S. service members have been wounded [1].

In a separate incident highlighting the chaotic nature of the air war, three U.S. fighter jets were mistakenly shot down by Kuwaiti air defenses Sunday night, though all six aircrew ejected safely [2][3].

### High-Profile Iranian Casualties
The operation has eliminated key Iranian leadership, including Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, who was killed on the first day with Israeli targeting assistance from the U.S. [1]. Other confirmed deaths include Ali Shamkhani (secretary of Iran's Defense Council), Mohammed Pakpour (IRGC commander), Defense Minister Amir Nasirzadeh, and Abdolrahim Mousavi (chief of staff of armed forces) [4]. Israel assessed that a "majority of the highest-ranking senior military officials of the Iranian security leadership" were eliminated [4].

### Civilian Casualties
The conflict has produced substantial civilian casualties, particularly in Iran. The most severe single incident occurred when a missile struck a girls' elementary school in Minab, Iran, reportedly killing at least 175 people, many of them children [1]. The school was in session due to Iran's Saturday-Sunday workweek, though classes had been canceled shortly before the strike [1].

Civilian death toll estimates vary significantly between sources. Iran's Red Crescent reports at least 555 total deaths since the war began [1], while the Human Rights Activist News Agency (HRANA) provides a "minimum reliable estimate" of 742 civilian deaths with nearly 1,000 injured as of Monday [1]. Additional strikes have hit hospitals and schools in Tehran [1].

### Military Objectives and Retaliation
The U.S. has achieved several stated military objectives, including sinking portions of Iran's navy and striking Iranian ballistic missile and nuclear facilities [1]. Iran has retaliated with missile and drone strikes against Israel and Gulf states, including attacks near Kuwait's international airport and U.S. facilities [2][3].

### Political Context
Public support for the operation appears limited, with a Reuters/Ipsos poll showing only 25% of Americans supporting the strikes and 43% disapproving [4]. This comes as Trump's overall approval rating has declined, with 60% disapproving of his presidency in pre-operation polling [4].
